alkylation reagent: alkylated with 2‐Chloroacetamide (50 mM) for 30 min at room temperature in the dark.
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 2‐Chloroacetamide (50 mM) for 30 min at room temperature in the dark.
cleavage agent: predigested with LysC (1 μg/100 μg of sample) for 2 h at 37°C.
collision energy: The 10 most intense peaks were selected for higher‐energy collision dissociation (HCD) fragmentation at 30% of normalized collision energy.
enrichment method: TiO2 phosphopeptide enrichment, ERLIC chromatography, and LC–MS/MS.
fragmentation method: HCD fragmentation at 30% of normalized collision energy.
instrument: the raw data were acquired on the mass spectrometer in a data‐dependent acquisition mode (DDA). Full‐scan MS spectra were acquired in the Orbitrap.
ionization type: mass spectrometer equipped with an Easy‐Spray source.
labeling: we performed biological replicates of SILAC phosphoproteomics in HeLa cells treated with DMSO or DRB for 30 min.
reduction reagent: cysteines were reduced with of TCEP (10 mM) for 30 min at room temperature.
reduction concentration: cysteines were reduced with of TCEP (10 mM) for 30 min at room temperature.
